pain_point_label: Format and Requirement Drift Across Agency Updates

matches.match_rationale: The core problem is that multi-site compliance teams fail to detect and interpret regulatory updates from DepEd, BIR, and other agencies in time. A research agent can autonomously monitor agency portals, gazette publications, and official circulars, then synthesize exactly what changed (field definitions, format revisions, new validations) and surface actionable diffs to compliance officers. This directly addresses the 'missed update' failure mode specific to Philippine education operators dealing with multiple agencies.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An always-on agent that scrapes DepEd, BIR, and CHED update feeds weekly, compares new memoranda against the current filing requirements baseline, and emails a structured 'what changed and what to re-check' brief to compliance leads before the next submission cycle.

matches.match_rationale: Compliance staff frequently need to answer definitional questions — 'Is this student's fee a scholarship or a discount under the current BIR circular?' — but the authoritative answer shifts as agency interpretations change. A RAG system grounded in the latest official circulars, memoranda, and issuances lets staff query definitions and get cited, version-aware answers rather than relying on memory or outdated internal guides. This directly addresses the definitional misinterpretation failure mode.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance Q&A tool where staff type 'how does DepEd classify partial fee waivers in Form X as of the latest memo?' and get a cited answer drawn from an up-to-date document corpus of agency issuances, with the source circular referenced so reviewers can verify.

matches.match_rationale: When an agency changes field definitions or submission schemas, the existing internal data (e.g., student financial records) must be re-mapped to the new format before re-filing. AI-assisted schema mapping can automatically translate existing structured data from the old format to the new agency-mandated format, reducing manual rework per site. For a 500+ staff multi-site operator running several campuses, this eliminates the manual re-tagging of hundreds of records per revision cycle.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A data transformation layer that ingests the operator's internal student-fee data and, given a new agency submission template, auto-maps fields and flags records where the new classification rules are ambiguous (e.g., rows that could be either 'scholarship' or 'discount' under the updated definition).

matches.match_rationale: The full re-filing workflow — detecting a format change, pulling the current data, remapping to the new template, validating completeness, and routing for sign-off — is a multi-step process that today is manual and error-prone across sites. A task automation agent can orchestrate this entire workflow: trigger on a detected regulatory update, pull records from the student information system, apply the new field mappings, run validation checks, and queue the output for human review before submission. This directly addresses cascading re-filing overhead.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agent-driven compliance pipeline that, upon receiving a new DepEd or BIR circular, automatically prepares a draft re-submission package aligned to the updated format, surfaces only the records that require human judgment (ambiguous classifications), and routes them to the responsible compliance officer per campus.

matches.match_rationale: A recurring source of rejections is misclassification of fee types (scholarship vs discount, full waiver vs partial subsidy) according to current agency definitions. A text classifier trained on the latest agency definitions can automatically label each student fee record against the current taxonomy, reducing human misinterpretation and catching edge cases before submission. This is especially valuable when the label definitions change and must be re-applied across thousands of historical records.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A fee-type classification model that takes student fee record descriptions and applies the current DepEd/BIR taxonomy, flagging records where confidence is low or where the label would differ from the prior submission cycle — giving compliance staff a targeted review queue rather than a full audit.
